title: Brazil Launches 5th Edition of Plan to Reduce COâ‚‚ Emissions in the Airline Sector
content: Brazil has just released the 5th edition of the Action Plan for the Reduction of COâ‚‚ Emissions in Brazilian Civil Aviation. The launch took place during the 42nd Assembly of the International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO), which takes place from September 23 to October 3, in MontrÃ©al, Canada. The document is part of international efforts and Brazil's compliance with its obligations to ICAO to reduce COâ‚‚ emissions in civil aviation. The Federal Government's initiative is coordinated by the Ministry of Ports and Airports, with the participation of the National Civil Aviation Agency (Anac), the Department of Airspace Control (Decea) and representatives of the airline industry. The new edition of the Action Plan for the Reduction of COâ‚‚ Emissions in Brazilian Civil Aviation brings important news, such as the incorporation of the Fuel of the Future Law (Law 14,993/24), which establishes the staggered use of sustainable aviation fuels (SAF) in the country, determining that domestic operations will adopt 1% SAF as of 2027, reaching at least 10% in 2037.
author: Analysts Account
